title: Eva Unit 03 and its Goro arms
display_title: Eva Unit 03 and its Goro arms
wikitext:
[]: Eva Unit-03 and its Goro-arms             Quote Post by unit450 » Mon Jun 21, 2010 1:39 am So was wondering about this for a while now, but while Eva Unit-03 suddenly sprouts Goro-arms in the middle of it's 15 minutes of fame. Was the extra arms included during the unit's construction, and Bardiel just took control of them as well? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 21, 2010 2:04 am I like the theory, but there's a couple of problems. First off, Eva-03 isn't a testbed. It's described like this on the poster: Ultimate All-Purpose Humanoid Decisive Battle Weapon - Artificial Human Evangelion - Regular Use Model 03 EVANGELION PRODUCTION MODEL-03 This is identical to how Eva-02 is described. Interestingly, in Rebuild, the implication is that Eva-02 and Eva-03 are "Mass Production Models" (a note associated with Eva-02 states this). Also, the reason there isn't a major Nerv base in Canada is because the US demanded the rights to two bases and two Evas as part of the agreements. Germany got this concession as well (Nerv-Hamburg and Nerv-Berlin, and building Eva-02 and at least one MP Eva, possibly two). It's possible Canada built an MP Eva, btw. There were seven bases used, but only Germany and China are mentioned, and between them they only seem to have three Nerv bases.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jun 21, 2010 2:11 am Ah don't worry about Canada too much I was always wondering what has happened to my country (Italy) and I've discovered that it still exist only thanks to the Vatican (if the name wasn't used just for his coolness) and the first character of Italian origins in the Evangelion universe has appeared in the Gakuen Datenroku manga that I don't even care to read (it was my bro to tell me about it) unless I've missed something. Also U.S.A. have both the super secret Area 51 in Nevada (that was probably the place where EVA-04 was developed and the famous M.I.T. where EVA-03 was developed (also U.S.A. insisted about developing them and they have the power to be persuasive). Anyway back to the topic, it's somehow implied that Angels can evolve themselves to the greatest degree when they're embryos but they can improve their abilities even after they're mature (for example Sachiel's second face) and so I doubt highly that EVA-03 originally had those "hidden" arms. Actually 03 didn't look like a test-bed, it was probably quite similar to 02 (the Seele refers to it as a "State of the Art Weapon", not as a new prototype) and if it was being used to test something new (I highly doubt it) surely the Seele removed it before sending it to the Nerv HQ. Edit: started writing before Legendary posted, I'm sorry for being a slowpoke.

[]:             Quote Post by unit450 » Mon Jun 21, 2010 4:48 am Your right the American Eva's at least Unit-03 were not testbeds, Eva Unit-04 while a production model was just a test subject for the S2 engine though. Still the production models were not just base combat units either, and could be subject to modification anyways; like Unit-02's Beast Mode. Legendary wrote: Interestingly, in Rebuild, the implication is that Eva-02 and Eva-03 are "Mass Production Models" (a note associated with Eva-02 states this).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 21, 2010 5:07 am I'm pretty sure that was just an angel shape-shifting sort of deal, just as the stretchy arms were, and not a NERV product feature. Also, I thought we already settled that the 'staples' are actually stitches (hence why the mostly span 'seams' between uninfected bits)... sort of like Asuka's doll. I get the definite feeling that Asuka was, at some level, an active participant in that fight.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jun 21, 2010 5:39 am unit450 wrote: Rebuild Eva Unit-03 seems to have been contructed in Arizona, can't be sure exactly; but it might've been close to Unit-04 even if so. They've had great regenerative abilities(in Sachiel's case), but I haven't seen evolution; maybe Zereul when it absorbs Rei/Unit-00 to gain easy entry to Dogma. Gotta admit I totally forgot about this scene between Gendo and Seele, but I think it helps my question a bit actually. State of the Art usually refers to something more advanced than whats commonly used in the field; such as more features, less restrictions, ease of use/maintenace), and Gendo says he could not trust an untested unit as well. Well I don't see why they would remove them actually, but my idea of the arms being dormant is quashed now though. Since they may have been trying to 'advertise' Unit-03's features during that conversation.

[]: Re: Eva Unit-03 and its ... [Spoilers!]             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Jun 21, 2010 9:11 am unit450 wrote: So was wondering about this for a while now, but while Eva Unit-03 suddenly sprouts Goro-arms in the middle of it's 15 minutes of fame. Was the extra arms included during the unit's construction, and Bardiel just took control of them as well?

[]:             Quote Post by The Fourth Evangelion » Mon Jun 21, 2010 9:52 am unit450 wrote: Your right the American Eva's at least Unit-03 were not testbeds, Eva Unit-04 while a production model was just a test subject for the S2 engine though. Still the production models were not just base combat units either, and could be subject to modification anyways; like Unit-02's Beast Mode.

[]:             Quote Post by unit450 » Mon Jun 21, 2010 9:54 am Yea all these responses are most likely right, thanks guys was just just voicing a wild theory(we each get one or two right?) Azathoth wrote: I'd say that Mark 06 has usurped the role of the Eva Series: the Eva that SEELE created on their own, under their (relatively) direct control, to initiate Third Impact and Human Instrumentality on their own terms without the need for NERV. I would be quite surprised if the harpies turned back up now, actually.

[]: Re: Eva Unit-03 and its ... [Spoilers!]             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jun 21, 2010 10:03 am Azathoth wrote: In NGE, yes. In Rebuild? Nope. S2 Engine doesn't exist anymore, remember? (Or at least that's the direction in which all the evidence is pointing.) This leads me to wonder, yet again, what exactly happened with Unit 04, both in NGE and in Rebuild. I suppose, given the crossplosion, that we're meant to assume the core blew up...but it does seem rather a large explosion, doesn't it? Ah, well. Anno never gave us an answer about that before, why would he bother this time?

[]:             Quote Post by Rathalos » Wed Jun 23, 2010 6:29 pm When I first watched this fight scene, or should I say guro rape scene, between Unit 1 and Unit 3, I thought they were steps from a ladder that were "growing" out of Unit 1's neck.

[]:             Quote Post by Velefor » Wed Jul 21, 2010 1:01 am I was going to create my own Eva-03 discussion thread with blackjack and hookers but since I found this one I think that little forum necromancy will do the trick. So the question is: whose soul inhabited Unit-03. Of course we can’t be 100% sure that rebuild Evas function in a same way that their NGE colleges did, but it’s safe to assume they do. All other universes laws are pretty much identical in both, and we are heavily hinted that Yui-sama’s soul still dwells inside 01. If staff about soul-pilot synchronization is still accurate, how in the world can Unit-03 synchronize with Asuka or Rei (remember that first test lunch was supposed to be done by Rei until Asuka volunteered)? What soul would easily accept two completely different persons? The only wild guess that I could come up with is that they somehow have put the fraction of Lilith soul in there. Maybe since she is the mother of all humanity, she can synchronize with anyone. But there is a lot of major problems with such speculations, so don’t even consider it serious =( The other issue that I would like to bring is about those two extra hands. Does they really resemble Asuka’s two you or are they just average human looking angel hands? If their shape is indeed influenced by pilot’s mind and body trapped inside, the whole issue of two Evas going for each other’s throats becomes pretty ironic, doesn’t it? Especially taking into consideration how Sinji liked to solve his issues with Aska in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Wed Jul 21, 2010 1:39 pm Actually I don't think we can assume the same rules apply. Mari jumps in 02 with no problem, and it's been a while since I saw 1.0 but I think Rei can pilot 01. I know this has been mentioned on these forums before, but in the elevator scene in NME Rei says Eva's are mirrors of their soul, and don't have their own which is what she says in the original. Eva 01 probably has Yui since Gendou looks at it and says, "What up babycakes?" Otherwise the other Eva's have something else going on with them.

[]:             Quote Post by Velefor » Wed Jul 21, 2010 3:04 pm Lonecow12 wrote: Mari jumps in 02 with no problem

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Wed Jul 21, 2010 4:42 pm The general consensus on here is that the Mother Souls' are no longer canon in Rebuild, or at least until it is stated differently.

[]:             Quote Post by Velefor » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:06 am Interesting. I would be really grateful if someone would provide a link for a thread where such conclusion was reached.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Thu Jul 22, 2010 8:14 am Here is one of many: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=367153
